title: Other Economic Motives: The main motivation for mergers and acquisitions is always to...
summary: Evaluate whether synergistic gains are the sole motivation for M&A or if other factors also apply.
prompt: The main motivation for mergers and acquisitions is always to achieve synergistic gains.
solution: While synergistic gains are a significant motive, firms may also pursue M&A for other reasons such as expansion, financial opportunities, and tax motives. Each merger or acquisition can have multiple motives, and understanding these can provide deeper insights into the reasons behind a specific M&A transaction. Thus, synergy is not the sole reason for M&As.
